title: aquilegia formosa var. flavescens
intro: Aquilegia formosa var. flavescens, also known as the golden columbine, is a variant of the western columbine plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. The health benefits of golden columbine include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Golden columbine can be used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
